sections.title: Performance Analysis — Distill (book_shorts)
sections.content: I read through `content.ts`, `store.ts`/`progress.ts`/`prefs.ts`, `tts.ts`/`follow.ts`, and the three pages. Findings below, ordered by impact.

sections.title: 1. Store-wide re-render fan-out (biggest issue — "N+1 render" pattern)
2. Linear scans instead of indexed lookups (`content.ts`)
3. Redundant computation: `bookStats` recomputed every render, per card
4. `searchBooks` rebuilds search corpus on every keystroke
5. `loadChapter` re-parses markdown on every navigation
6. Minor / lower-impact
sections.content:
- **`BookMap.tsx:84-85`** — `MAP_SPEECH_SELECTOR` is a string literal re-created every render. Hoist it to module scope (like `Reader.tsx`'s `SPEECH_SELECTOR` should be too, `Reader.tsx:38-39`).
- **`follow.ts` `useSpeechFollow`** — `elementsRef` is included in the effect's dependency array; refs are stable so this is harmless but misleading — safe to drop it, no behavior change.
- **Potential leak edge case in `tts.ts`**: `startKeepalive` (`tts.ts:141`) sets a `window.setInterval`, cleared only via `stopKeepalive()` inside `finish()`/`ttsStop()`. Every call path you have does clean up correctly (including the page-level `useEffect(() => () => ttsStop(), [])` unmount guards in both `BookMap.tsx:81` and `Reader.tsx:92`), so this isn't currently leaking — but it's fragile: any future code path that flips `status` away from `'idle'`/`'playing'` without going through `ttsStop`/`finish` would leave the 10s interval running forever (module-level, so it'd survive route changes). Worth a comment or an assertion, not an active bug.
- **`Reader.tsx:100-109`** — the scroll listener effect depends on `[state]`, so it detaches/reattaches on every chapter load transition (`loading`→`ready`). Not expensive, but could depend on `[]` since `onScroll` doesn't capture anything from `state`.

sections.title: Suggested priority
sections.content:
1. Fix store fan-out (#1) — biggest win as the library grows, since it's O(books) wasted re-renders per progress write.
2. Add `Map`-based indices in `content.ts` (#2) and cache `bookStats`/`loadChapter` (#3, #5) — all mechanical, low-risk changes in one file.
3. Precompute search corpus (#4) — only matters once the catalog is larger than a handful of books, but it's a one-time, no-risk change.

None of these are urgent at the current catalog size (a handful of books/chapters), but #1 and #2 will matter as soon as the library grows past ~20-30 books, since they're the two patterns whose cost scales with catalog size on every interaction. Want me to implement any of these?
